page: UpCloud PREMIUM-2xCPU-4GB is a KVM-based virtual server configured with 2 shared vCPUs and 4.0 GB of RAM. The underlying hardware utilizes an AMD EPYC 9575F processor with an x86_64 architecture, operating at a base clock speed of 2.0 GHz. Each of the two physical cores supports a single thread. The processor features a cache hierarchy consisting of 64 KB L1d, 0.5 MB L2, and 16 MB L3 cache. It supports advanced instruction sets including AVX-512 vector extensions, AVX_VNNI, and AVX512_BF16, alongside hardware-accelerated cryptography via AES, VAES, and SHA_NI. The server is bundled with 50 GB of local SSD storage and includes one complimentary public IPv4 address.

Benchmark results reveal a stark contrast between single-core and multi-core execution. The server achieves top-tier single-core CPU performance in stress-ng tests, placing in the top 10% of recorded results, which is mirrored by top-tier single-threaded gzip compression and bzip3 decompression speeds. However, multi-core performance is average, and overall PassMark CPU and memory scores fall into the weak and poor categories, respectively. Memory bandwidth for small cached blocks is weak, though large block read and write bandwidths remain average. Database and key-value operations show limitations, with PassMark database operations and Redis SET performance ranking in the bottom 25%. Static web serving throughput and small-model LLM inference speeds sit in the average tier.

From a cost-efficiency perspective, the shared CPU allocation model provides access to modern AMD EPYC architecture at a lower entry point, though resource contention may affect sustained multi-threaded performance. The 1:2 vCPU-to-RAM ratio makes this instance suitable for workloads that benefit from strong single-threaded burst performance but do not require massive memory footprints or sustained multi-core execution. Suitable workloads include lightweight web serving, single-threaded batch processing, basic development environments, and small-scale utility services.
description: UpCloud PREMIUM-2xCPU-4GB is a KVM-based virtual server featuring 2 shared vCPUs powered by an AMD EPYC 9575F processor on the x86_64 architecture, paired with 4.0 GB of RAM and 50 GB of local SSD storage. The CPU operates at 2.0 GHz and supports AVX-512 and cryptographic instruction sets. In benchmark evaluations, the server demonstrates top-tier single-core CPU performance and single-threaded compression speeds, though its multi-core performance and memory bandwidth are average to weak. PassMark database and Redis SET operations also rank in the lower quartiles. This resource profile offers a budget-friendly entry point to modern CPU architecture, making it suitable for workloads that prioritize single-threaded execution over sustained multi-core throughput, such as lightweight web serving, development environments, and basic utility tasks.
og_description: UpCloud PREMIUM-2xCPU-4GB is a KVM virtual server with 2 shared AMD EPYC vCPUs, 4 GB RAM, and 50 GB SSD storage, offering strong single-core performance alongside average multi-core capabilities.

tagline: A budget-friendly virtual server combining strong single-threaded AMD EPYC performance with shared vCPUs and local SSD storage.
bullet_points: 2 shared vCPUs powered by AMD EPYC 9575F processor
4.0 GB RAM and 50 GB local SSD storage included
Top-tier single-core CPU and single-threaded compression performance
Average static web serving and small-model LLM inference speeds
Suited for lightweight web serving and development environments
